page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n2.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores, running 1.0 thread per core. It includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates that the ecs.n2.large sits in the lower performance tiers for most compute and memory operations. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng fall into the bottom 25 percent, with scores of 1089.08 and 4315.32 respectively. Memory read bandwidth is also weak, measuring 313.13 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 32.61 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, large block memory write bandwidth performs at an average level of 28.16 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression benchmarks are poor, placing in the bottom 10 percent of tested systems.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the ecs.n2.large is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, such as basic web serving, development environments, and low-traffic database operations. Its PassMark database score of 1111.04 and Redis SET performance of 5.02 million operations per second reflect its suitability for non-intensive application tiers. Qualitatively, the instance offers a dedicated CPU allocation with standard memory density, making it a predictable option for workloads that do not require high-performance compute or rapid data processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n2.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the KVM hypervisor. The x86_64 architecture includes AVX-512 instruction support but lacks bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark evaluations place the instance primarily in the lower performance tiers, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU metrics falling in the bottom 25 percent. Memory read bandwidth is similarly weak, though large-block memory write speeds reach average levels at 28.16 GB/sec. With a PassMark database score of 1111.04 and a Redis throughput of 5.02 million operations per second, this instance is best suited for lightweight, non-intensive workloads such as basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale database operations where dedicated CPU allocation is preferred over raw processing speed.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n2.large is a general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, suitable for lightweight workloads and basic web serving.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance with balanced memory and compute resources for lightweight applications and development environments.
